story.text: As bdo's session, I need an honest, hands-on verdict on whether Causality has the editing, data, and persistence to be a usable tool or is still a toy — run live in a real browser against real local inference, not read off the code — because a beautiful demo can hide the absence of working memory.

briefing.headline: Causality feature audit: toy-vs-tool, run live
briefing.value: The browser-driven companion to the desk-read reality audit (PR #114): the foundry canvas served, infer-bridge started with real ollama inference, canvas.html driven through its __cz debug handle with screenshots at each state. The verdict is grounded in observed behavior, not inferred from source.
briefing.why_now: bdo asked to launch Causality and run a quality scan and feature audit. It stacks on #114 (the causality/ module); landed after it, this reduces to the single audit doc.
briefing.if_accepted: A grounded verdict — a proven grammar with a beautiful body and no working memory: today a toy, an extraordinary one. Editing shallow (only the label is editable; the prompt that defines an inference node is invisible). Data half-real (inference works end-to-end; the canvas has no ingress of the real ontum log). Persistence absent and decisive (no save/load/export; every reload is total loss). Plus a prioritized punch list mapped onto epic.causality-surface's own waves.
briefing.if_rejected: Causality's toy-vs-tool gap stays a felt impression instead of an evidenced punch list, and the P0 changes that flip the verdict stay unprioritized.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low: the audit is a document — observation written down with embedded evidence; it moves no truth and gates nothing.
briefing.mechanism: causality/audit/feature-audit.md, the hands-on companion to reality-audit.md. Evidence embedded inline (screenshots foundry-side at experience-foundry/shots/audit-01..05) so the doc stands alone; the punch list maps to the epic's waves rather than inventing a new plan.

_note: Birth record for the Causality feature audit, authored to adopt PR #118 into the loop (bdo, 2026-06-13). Stacks on atom.causality-term-economy.v0. Never mutated; verdicts are the fold over .ai-native/log/ (D-5).
